title: Learning inside the test loop
summary: This issue focuses on where machine learning genuinely helps in test: decision ordering in ATPG, congestion surrogates in physical design, and the reproducibility harness that keeps results honest.

title: Chiplets, and how to test them
summary: A deep dive into testability-aware partitioning, known-good-die verification, and why die-to-die test access changes the packaging calculus.

title: Trust you can scan for
summary: Hardware security through infrastructure you already have: reusing scan and compression for Trojan detection, plus notes on side-channel resilience.

title: Open silicon, reproducible results
summary: Why we build on open PDKs and open EDA, and how OpenFlow-X makes a full RTL-to-GDS flow something anyone can rerun and audit.

title: Sparsity all the way down
summary: From structured-sparse models to a datapath that skips zero work — the SparseCore approach to efficient inference.

title: Frontiers: DNA and hybrid substrates
summary: A look at the lab's exploratory programs — molecular storage with HelixStore and hybrid analog/digital computing.
